slides.title: Your arteries are meant to *stay soft*.
slides.sub: With age they harden, and each heartbeat hits harder. Here is how to keep them springy.

slides.title: Soft pipes, calm pressure
slides.body: Young arteries flex with every beat, cushioning the flow. Over time they stiffen, and that cushion *slowly fades away*.

slides.title: Pulse-wave velocity
slides.body: How fast your pulse travels is a marker of stiffness. A faster wave is *linked to* harder, less elastic arteries.

slides.title: Delicate organs take the hit
slides.body: Stiff arteries let each beat pound downstream. That extra force is *linked to* strain on the brain and kidneys.

slides.title: Move your heart often
slides.body: Regular aerobic exercise, like brisk walks or cycling, *can support* more elastic arteries and steadier pressure over the years.

slides.title: Ease off the salt
slides.body: Lower sodium is linked to softer arteries and *calmer blood pressure*. Cooking at home is the simplest place to start.

slides.title: Keep every beat *gentle*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the movement, steps, food and sleep behind elastic arteries, and rolls it into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Arteries are supposed to flex with every heartbeat. With age they harden, and that pressure travels straight to your brain and kidneys. 🌿

Aerobic movement and less salt are linked to softer, more elastic arteries and calmer pressure. Save this for the next gym day or grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How to Keep Your Arteries Young as You Age
